title: Editing Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the editing features — entering edit mode, using the toolbar, making changes, and saving to GitHub. You'll need to be signed in to follow along.

title: Navigate to Vocabularies
description: First, let's head to the vocabularies page to pick a vocabulary to edit.

title: Pick a Vocabulary
description: Click a vocabulary card to open it. We'll use this one for our editing walkthrough.

title: Edit Button
description: The Edit dropdown lets you choose between Full and Inline edit modes. Full mode opens a form-based editor; Inline mode lets you click cells directly in the concept table.

title: Enter Inline Edit Mode
description: We'll switch to inline edit mode now. This adds editing controls to the toolbar and makes concept properties editable directly in the table.

title: The Edit Toolbar
description: The toolbar provides all the editing controls — mode switching, undo/redo, change tracking, view options, and save. Let's look at each one.

title: Mode Switch
description: Toggle between Full and Inline edit modes. Full mode shows a form-based editor; Inline lets you edit values directly in the table.

title: Undo & Redo
description: Made a mistake? Use undo to step back through your changes, or redo to restore them. Each action is tracked individually.

title: Select a Concept
description: Click a concept in the tree to select it. In inline mode, its properties become editable in the detail panel.

title: Inline Editing
description: Click any editable cell to modify its value. Changes are tracked locally until you save them to GitHub.

title: Add a Concept
description: Use the Add button to create a new concept in the vocabulary. It will appear at the top of the tree ready for you to fill in.

title: Pending Changes
description: The changes badge shows how many concepts you've modified. Click it to see a summary of all pending changes, with the option to revert individual items.

title: View Mode
description: Switch between Simple and Expert views. Simple hides technical properties like IRIs; Expert shows everything.

title: Save to GitHub
description: When you're done editing, click Save. You'll enter a commit message, then the changes are pushed to the GitHub repository as a new commit.

title: Exit Edit Mode
description: Click the close button to leave edit mode and return to the browse view. Unsaved changes will prompt for confirmation.

title: Editor Tour Complete!
description: You've seen the full editing workflow — entering edit mode, making changes inline, tracking modifications, and saving to GitHub. Start this tour again anytime from the header.
